channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As Jean-Marie Le Pen is buried, here's a look at key dates of the rise of the far right in France
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s main far-right party and a polarizing figure in French politics, is being buried Saturday in a private family ceremony in his hometown of La Trinité-sur-Mer in Brittany.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Californians scrambled to evacuate dogs, cats, horses, even pigs as LA flames closed in
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Arianna Buturovic kept a wary eye on distant smoke from the rescue shelter she runs outside Los Angeles for dogs at risk of being euthanized. Within hours, nearby mountains were ablaze and fire began encircling her.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Winter weather limits North Carolina governor's inauguration to TV speech
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — With an outdoor public inauguration ceremony canceled due to winter weather, new North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein instead planned to kick off his term in office with a televised address indoors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: A winter storm that dropped heavy snow and glazed roads throughout the U.S. South is maintaining its icy grip on much of the region into the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback And Travis Loller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Venezuela latest: Maduro sworn in for third term as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Flames were already attacking the campus of Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center when the cantor, Ruth Berman Harris, and three companions rushed in to rescue its sacred Torah scrolls.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire chief urges public to trust alerts despite false warning
item.description: "Our goal is not to have erroneous alerts go out," said Kenichi Haskett, section chief for the LA County Fire Department.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: LA Rams head for Arizona and a crucial playoff game with their minds on their fire-ravaged hometown
item.description: The Los Angeles Rams wrapped up practice at their training complex Friday under blue skies with bruise-colored clouds of wildfire smoke lurking on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 11 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: Disney says it will donate $15 million to respond to the fires and help rebuild. The company announced the donations Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge sentences Trump to unconditional discharge, no punishment in hush money conviction
item.description: President-elect Trump was spared any punishment for his hush money criminal conviction Friday when a New York judge sentenced him to an unconditional discharge, eschewing jail time while securing his status as the first person convicted of a felony to assume the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Look at their faces — formal, etched with experience, laden with the weight of momentous decisions. Add up their years: 379 to be exact, enough time to take you back into the mid-1600s, when the notion of the American nation was still more than a century away.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump faces sentencing in New York case after SCOTUS fails to intervene and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas Raider Charles Snowden thought he was in Florida during DUI arrest: Video
item.description: Tests showed Snowden’s blood-alcohol level was more than double the legal limit after officers found him asleep and unresponsive in a running SUV, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump tells GOP he wants to write 100 executive orders
item.description: “He threw that out — 100 — there could be like 100 EOs, yeah. I believe him.” Sen. Kevin Cramer (R-N.D.) told The Hill, referring to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Laken Riley Act overwhelmingly clears first hurdle in Senate with help from Democrats
item.description: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the Laken Riley Act, a bill aimed at curbing crime by migrants, in the first vote of the year by the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Could Bakersfield see wind-driven fires like those in LA? Absolutely
item.description: The urban wildfires now devastating Los Angeles County have been driven largely by winds that in some cases have reached hurricane force.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, NewsNation

item.title: Officer Michael Horan honored with funeral after being killed in the line of duty
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — The community came together to celebrate the life of a Greensboro police officer, just over two weeks after he was killed in the line of duty. Officer Michael Horan's funeral was held at Westover Church in Greensboro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, NewsNation

item.title: Lawsuit by New Orleans truck attack victims says city, contractors failed to implement safety system
item.description: Six people who were injured and the father of a man who was killed in the New Year's truck attack filed a lawsuit Thursday against the City of New Orleans and two contractors, claiming they failed to protect revelers from an Army veteran who sped around a police blockade and raced down Bourbon Stree
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah firefighters answer the call to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: After a quick briefing, they hit the road to begin a 12-hour drive to California to battle the deadly and devastating Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned that there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Lawyers collecting $148 million judgment from Rudy Giuliani say World Series rings must be given up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for two former Georgia election workers who are owed $148 million in damages after suing Rudy Giuliani for defamation said Tuesday that evidence proves their clients are entitled to three World Series rings that the former New York City mayor says he gave to his son.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sole survivor of West Valley murder-suicide expected to be released from hospital soon
item.description: The sole survivor of a murder-suicide in West Valley City that left five family members dead is expected to be released from the hospital soon.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
